what_it_does: Defines an HTML page that loads shared styles and runtime scripts, fetches layout and decision JSON, selects the form-taxonomy decision, renders it as a composed decision-card slide, and appends it to #mount.
observations: The page title identifies the view as Decision — form-taxonomy.
The body contains only a #mount element plus runtime script loading and an inline async renderer.
Inline CSS establishes a warm full-height page and makes #mount an inline-size container.
The decision id is hardcoded as form-taxonomy.
The script fetches /dna/layouts.json and /content/decisions.json in parallel.
The rendered identity includes a custom decision://global/form-taxonomy address and pending state.
The explanation_source resolver reads rec.legibility.why when available.
The final rendered view receives the dv-c class before insertion.
standouts: This is a data-driven page shell for one decision id.
The interface and explanatory slide are composed through DNA.renderExplained.
The custom address, pending state, visual device, and explanation resolver are wired at render time.
conventions: Uses shared /surface styles and runtime scripts instead of defining component markup in the file.
Uses an async IIFE for page initialization.
Keeps the HTML shell minimal and delegates rendering to DNA runtime functions.
Uses container-type:inline-size to support container-based layout behavior.
concerns: No local handling for failed fetches, missing decision records, or missing archetype data.
The hardcoded id and address must stay aligned manually.
The resolver returns empty strings for unrecognized names, which may hide unexpected resolver requests.
notes: This file is a thin page entry point for rendering the form-taxonomy decision card as a composed slide.
questions: What should happen if the form-taxonomy record is absent from decisions.json?
What resolver names besides explanation_source and state can the archetype request?
Is pending always the intended state for this rendered decision page?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated composed decision view purpose matches the implementation: it renders a contained decision interface with slide explanation around the form-taxonomy record.
intention_signals: It seems intended to instantiate a reusable decision-card renderer for a single decision substrate while keeping content, layout, and visual-device choices externalized.
novelty: The file combines a custom decision URI, archetype rendering, visual-device derivation, and explanation-slide composition in a minimal static HTML entry point.
contribution: Contributes a file-local entry point that binds the form-taxonomy decision id to shared DNA layout/content data, preserving address, state, visual-device, explanation-source, and composed-slide wiring for a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: HTML entry point that renders the form-taxonomy decision as a composed DNA decision-card slide from shared layout and decision JSON data.
intention_for_embedding: This was for presenting one addressed decision substrate through a reusable composed decision-card interface and explanatory slide renderer.
